description.task: Create a BankAccount class that uses an instance field for balance and a static field to track the total number of accounts created.
instructions: Implement the constructor: assign owner and balance, then increment totalAccounts.
Implement displayInfo(): print 'Owner: Alice | Balance: 1000.0 | Total Accounts: 3'.
Observe that all three objects share the same totalAccounts value.
description.hint: Static members belong to the class, not to any object. Every time a new BankAccount is created, the shared static counter increments.
description.expectedOutput:
Owner: Alice | Balance: 1000.0 | Total Accounts: 3
Owner: Bob | Balance: 500.0 | Total Accounts: 3
Owner: Carol | Balance: 750.0 | Total Accounts: 3

description.task: Create a MathUtils class with two static methods: one that returns the maximum of three integers and one that checks if a number is even.
description.context: Static methods belong to the class itself and can be called without creating an object. Utility methods that don't rely on object state are typically static.
instructions: Implement static int max(int a, int b, int c) that returns the largest of the three values.
Implement static boolean isEven(int n) that returns true if n % 2 == 0.
Call both methods using the class name (MathUtils.max(...)) — do not create an object.
description.hint: Use Math.max(a, Math.max(b, c)) or a chain of if statements to find the max of three values.
description.expectedOutput:
Max of 12, 7, 19: 19
Max of 5, 5, 3: 5
Is 8 even? true
Is 13 even? false

description.task: Create a Rectangle class with instance fields (width, height), an instance method for area, and a static method that creates a square.
instructions: Implement the constructor accepting width and height.
Implement getArea() as an instance method returning width * height.
Implement the static factory method square(double side) that returns new Rectangle(side, side).
description.hint: A static factory method creates and returns an object of its own class. It can call 'new Rectangle(...)' even though it's static.
